data.title: CN Logistics Clarifies Dividend Details in 2025 Annual Report
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from CN Logistics International Holdings Limited ( (HK:2130) ).

CN Logistics International Holdings Limited issued a clarification regarding its 2025 annual report, correcting a clerical error in the stated details of its proposed final dividend. The board has recommended a final dividend of HK1 cent per share for FY2025, totaling HK$3,004,890, subject to shareholder approval at the annual general meeting on 29 May 2026.

The company confirmed that, pending approval, the dividend is expected to be paid on 17 July 2026 to shareholders on the register as of 26 June 2026, and that all other information in the 2025 annual report remains unchanged. The announcement indicates that the correction is limited to clarifying the dividend amount and timetable, with no broader impact on the group’s reported results or governance structure.

More about CN Logistics International Holdings Limited

CN Logistics International Holdings Limited, listed in Hong Kong, operates in the logistics sector, providing freight forwarding and related logistics services for international trade. The company serves global customers that rely on efficient supply chain and cargo handling solutions, positioning itself as part of Hong Kong’s broader logistics and trading hub.

data.title: Plover Bay Wins Full Shareholder Backing for AGM Resolutions and Capital Mandates
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Plover Bay Technologies Ltd. ( (HK:1523) ).

Plover Bay Technologies Ltd. reported that all resolutions at its 28 April 2026 annual general meeting were approved by shareholders via poll, including the adoption of the 2025 audited financial statements and the reappointment of Ernst & Young as auditors for 2026. Shareholders also backed the re-election of long-serving independent non-executive directors, authorised the board to set directors’ pay, and granted mandates to issue up to 20% and repurchase up to 10% of share capital, as well as extend the issuance mandate by the amount repurchased.

The strong support across all resolutions, with each ordinary resolution receiving well over 50% of votes cast, reinforces the existing governance framework and gives the board wider flexibility in managing the company’s capital structure. These mandates position Plover Bay to respond more nimbly to future financing or capital management needs, while the continuity of its independent directors and auditors underpins stability and oversight for shareholders.

More about Plover Bay Technologies Ltd.

Plover Bay Technologies Ltd., incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates in the technology sector and is overseen by a board comprising executive and independent non-executive directors. The company targets global markets with its technology solutions and maintains a conventional Hong Kong-listed corporate governance structure with external auditors and a share registrar.

data.title: GenFleet Asserts Independent IP for Pan RAS Inhibitor GFH276 Amid Sector Dispute
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Genfleet Therapeutics (Shanghai), Inc. Class H ( (HK:2595) ) has provided an announcement.

GenFleet Therapeutics has issued a voluntary statement asserting that its Pan RAS inhibitor candidate GFH276 is based on fully independent and proprietary intellectual property, distancing itself from reported patent and trade secret disputes involving Revolution Medicines and a third party. The company says none of the entities or products mentioned in those disputes are related to GenFleet, and it has built a global patent portfolio for its Pan RAS program, including a core compound patent for GFH276 published in March 2025 with protection expected through 2044.

GFH276 is described as an oral, novel small-molecule Pan RAS (ON) inhibitor that hijacks cyclophilin A to target active, GTP-bound RAS proteins across most wild-type and mutant subtypes, including common KRAS mutations such as G12C, G12D and G12V. Preclinical data indicate dose-dependent antitumor activity and tumor regression in multiple KRAS-mutant models, and the company positions GFH276 as having potential to outperform mainstream switch II pocket-based KRAS inhibitors, underscoring its strategic push to strengthen its competitive position in the oncology drug development landscape.

More about Genfleet Therapeutics (Shanghai), Inc. Class H

GenFleet Therapeutics (Shanghai) Inc. is a China-based biopharmaceutical company focused on developing innovative small-molecule therapies targeting cancer. Its pipeline includes next-generation RAS pathway inhibitors aimed at a broad range of wild-type and mutant tumor types in global oncology markets.

Average Trading Volume: 2,225,832

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$18.32B

data.title: Edvantage Group’s Interim Profit Plunges on Lower Enrolments and Margin Pressure
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Edvantage Group Holdings Limited ( (HK:0382) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Edvantage Group Holdings Limited reported interim results for the six months ended 28 February 2026 showing softer operating performance amid a decline in student enrolments. Revenue slipped 4.9% year-on-year to RMB1.19 billion, while the number of students fell 7.1% to 93,170, highlighting pressure on the group’s core education business.

Profitability weakened more sharply as gross profit dropped 41.5% to RMB282.3 million and profit attributable to shareholders fell 58.4% to RMB101.5 million. Basic earnings per share declined nearly 60%, indicating a materially lower return to investors and suggesting that higher costs and lower utilisation are weighing on margins despite slightly lower selling and administrative expenses and reduced finance costs.

Total comprehensive income also contracted significantly compared with the prior-year period, reflecting both weaker profit and adverse foreign exchange movements. The results signal a more challenging operating environment for the group’s private education operations and may prompt closer scrutiny from shareholders regarding enrolment trends, cost control, and the sustainability of earnings.

More about Edvantage Group Holdings Limited

Edvantage Group Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-listed private education services provider incorporated in the Cayman Islands. The group operates schools and related education businesses, generating revenue primarily from tuition and education-related services for its enrolled student base in mainland China and associated markets.

Average Trading Volume: 1,375,907

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$1.16B

Samsonite International SA ( (HK:1910) ) has shared an announcement.

Samsonite Group S.A. plans to seek shareholder approval at its June 4, 2026 annual general meeting to appoint Sameer Suneja, Group CEO of confectionery giant Perfetti Van Melle Group B.V., as an independent non-executive director for a term running to the 2029 annual meeting. The proposed appointment, with annual remuneration of at least US$200,000 and confirmed compliance with Hong Kong independence criteria, would add a seasoned global consumer-goods executive to Samsonite’s board, potentially strengthening its governance depth and strategic consumer-market expertise.

Suneja brings nearly three decades of leadership and brand-building experience across multinational consumer businesses, including senior roles at Perfetti Van Melle in Europe and India and earlier positions with Frito-Lay India and Colgate-Palmolive (India) Ltd. Samsonite’s nomination and remuneration committees have endorsed his independence and compensation package, and a circular detailing the proposal and voting arrangements will be sent to shareholders ahead of the meeting.

data.title: Haier Smart Home Launches 2026 Plan to Boost Value, Global Growth and Efficiency
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Haier Smart Home Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:6690) ) has issued an update.

Haier Smart Home has unveiled its 2026 Corporate Value and Return Enhancement Action Plan, aiming to evolve from a traditional home appliance leader into a user-centric, platform-based technology ecosystem enterprise. The company is building a framework of one smart home channel, two customer and user experience platforms, and six core capabilities, including globalization, premiumization, digitalization, intelligentization, ecosystem integration, and greening.

On the revenue side, Haier plans to reinforce its leadership in white goods at home while targeting rapid growth in emerging overseas markets such as India, Southeast Asia, the Middle East, Africa, and Brazil. It is also accelerating the integration and expansion of its large HVAC business and cultivating emerging areas like smart home solutions, small appliances, healthcare and elderly care services, and recycling to diversify revenue and drive long-term growth.

On the efficiency side, the company is upgrading its operating model in China through a unified warehousing ToC system and an optimized logistics network to improve inventory turnover and terminal service capabilities. Overseas, Haier is focusing on brand building and upgrading to enhance operational efficiency and better capture global growth opportunities, supporting sustainable, high-quality development and improved investor returns.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:6690) stock is a Hold with a HK$23.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Haier Smart Home Co., Ltd. Class H stock, see the HK:6690 Stock Forecast page.

More about Haier Smart Home Co., Ltd. Class H

Haier Smart Home Co., Ltd. is a China-based global home appliance manufacturer focused on white goods, HVAC systems, and smart home solutions. The company targets both domestic and overseas markets, leveraging strengths in manufacturing, digitalization, and green, intelligent products to serve evolving consumer demand and expand its international footprint.

Standard Chartered has continued its share buyback programme, repurchasing 849,232 ordinary shares on 27 April 2026 via J.P. Morgan Securities at a volume-weighted average price of 1,757.2988 pence. The trades were executed across the London Stock Exchange and CBOE venues within pre-set parameters under applicable market abuse regulations.

Following the latest transaction, the bank has applied over US$753 million to the buyback to date and will cancel the repurchased shares, reducing its share count to 2,219,164,159. The cancellation tightens the free float and is expected to enhance earnings per share and capital efficiency, underscoring management’s confidence in the group’s financial position and long-term strategy.

Listed in London and Hong Kong, the bank focuses on cross-border financing, trade, and corporate banking, serving both institutional and individual clients in fast-growing markets.

An update from Tibet Water Resources ( (HK:1115) ) is now available.

5100 Xizang Glacier Company Limited has entered into a memorandum of understanding between its wholly owned unit Carry Rich Investment Limited and a research team led by Professor Yibin Feng at the School of Chinese Medicine, The University of Hong Kong. The collaboration will evaluate the clinical efficacy and safety of “5100” Xizang Glacier Mineral Water in patients with hyperuricemia and explore how its mineral components may affect uric acid metabolism and renal excretion.

Under the agreement, the university team will design and run randomized double-blind controlled trials in line with international clinical research standards, handle ethical approvals, and produce safety and efficacy analyses. Carry Rich will supply the mineral water, technical data, and logistical support, with the results expected to inform product application strategies and health management recommendations, potentially strengthening the brand’s positioning as a health-oriented functional water in the competitive beverage market.

More about Tibet Water Resources

5100 Xizang Glacier Company Limited, listed in Hong Kong, operates in the beverage industry and focuses on premium bottled mineral water under the “5100” Xizang Glacier brand. The Group supplies glacier mineral water products, with a market focus on health-conscious consumers seeking functional and high-end drinking water offerings.
